Package org.eventb.core.ast

Enum Class ProblemKind

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<ProblemKind>
org.eventb.core.ast.ProblemKind
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<ProblemKind>, Constable
public enum ProblemKind extends Enum<ProblemKind>
This enumeration contains all the problems that can be encountered by the various checkers.

When a sub-class of class ASTProblem is instantiated, it expects as parameters an unfixed number of arguments, depending on the problem you instantiate. Indicated here is the arguments needed with each problem. The index after the argument indicates its position in the arguments array.

Since:
1.0

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class java.lang.Enum

    Argument 1 is the name of the variable.

    No argument.
    DatatypeParsingError
    Argument 1 is the contents of the token which was expected
    DuplicateIdentifierInPattern

    No argument.
    ExtensionPreconditionError
    No argument.
    FreeIdentifierExpected
    No argument.
    FreeIdentifierHasBoundOccurences
    Argument 1 is the name of the variable.
    IncompatibleIdentExprNumbers
    Argument 1 is the number of assigned identifiers, argument 2 is the numbers of expressions.
    IncompatibleOperators
    Argument 1 is the image of the expected symbol, argument 2 is the name of the actual symbol.
    IntegerLiteralExpected
    No argument.
    InvalidAssignmentToImage
    No argument.
    InvalidGenericType
    Argument 1 is the expected type for the generic operator.
    InvalidTypeExpression
    No argument.
    LexerError
    Argument 1 is the contents of the token.
    MinusAppliedToSet
    No argument.
    MisplacedLedOperator
    Argument 1 is the image of the misplaced operator.
    MisplacedNudOperator
    Argument 1 is the image of the misplaced operator.
    MulAppliedToSet
    No argument.
    NotUpgradableError
    No argument.
    PredicateVariableNotAllowed
    Argument 1 is the illegal predicate variable.
    PrematureEOF
    No argument.
    TypeCheckFailure
    Non-located failure when type-checking.
    TypeNameUsedForRegularIdentifier
    Argument 1 is the name, argument 2 the alternative type.
    TypesDoNotMatch
    Argument 1 is the name of the first type, argument 2 is the name of the second type.
    TypeUnknown
    No argument.
    UnexpectedOftype
    No argument.
    UnexpectedSubFormulaKind
    Argument 1 is the name of the expected sub-formula kind, argument 2 is the name of the actual sub-formula kind.
    UnexpectedSymbol
    Argument 1 is the image of the expected symbol, argument 2 is the name of the actual symbol.
    UnknownOperator
    Argument 1 is the image of the unknown operator.
    UnmatchedTokens
    No argument.
    VariousPossibleErrors
    Deprecated.
    this error kind is no longer used.

  • Method Details

    • values

      public static ProblemKind[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
      Returns:
      an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared

    • valueOf

      public static ProblemKind valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null

    • makeCompoundMessage

      @Deprecated(since="3.8") public static String makeCompoundMessage(Collection<ASTProblem> problems)
      Deprecated.
      This method is no longer used.
      Returns a compound message from the given list of messages. Every single message is placed on a new line, indented with 2 spaces.
      Parameters:
      problems - a list of messages
      Returns:
      a string
      Since:
      2.0
